## design

- [Initial design — OSS CI/CD local stack](Initial.md) — The original design notes the repo grew from: Gitea + Woodpecker + Traefik compose stack, Caddy alternative, and the production-style .woodpecker.yml template (Python + K8s + Helm + Terraform + Vault).

## exploration

- [Forgejo vs Gitea — migration study](2026-05-30-forgejo-vs-gitea-migration-study.md) — DEFERRED point-in-time research record (2026-05-30): findings, flank-then-replace plan, risk audit, and open doubts for a possible Gitea → Forgejo migration. Not a committed decision.

## guide

- [Harbor container registry](HARBOR.md) — Setup and operation of the optional Harbor registry backend (REGISTRY_BACKEND=harbor): architecture, projects/robot accounts, Trivy scanning, and switching between Gitea and Harbor registries.
- [Platform access guide (agents)](PLATFORM-ACCESS.md) — How AI agents interact with the local siai stack: Gitea API tokens, Woodpecker API, docker registry commands, Playwright browser automation, and the Docker network architecture.

## learning

- [Pipeline clone DNS + tailnet reach (VM 107)](2026-05-28-pipeline-clone-dns.md) — Why every pipeline clone failed on the homelab stack: Docker substitutes public DNS for the loopback resolver (no MagicDNS in pipeline containers) + a missing tailnet ACL grant to ts-siai-gitea:443. Fixed at platform layer for all repos.
- [Woodpecker: service-under-test exits 137 at teardown](2026-06-19-woodpecker-service-under-test.md) — A commands:-based service runs under a pipe shell as PID 1 that never forwards SIGTERM → SIGKILL → exit 137. Fix: entrypoint exec-form so the server is PID 1; WOODPECKER_FORCE_IGNORE_SERVICE_FAILURE is the server-wide green-icon override.

## reference

- [Woodpecker pipeline notes](pipeline-notes.md) — Expected repo layout and Woodpecker UI secrets for the production pipeline template (.woodpecker.yml): registry, Vault, AWS/Terraform, kubeconfig.

## runbook

- [Onboard a repository to siai CI/CD](onboard-ci-consumer.md) — Step-by-step onboarding of a real repo to the homelab stack (Gitea → Woodpecker → Harbor on VM 107): activate, trust, secrets, pipeline template. Canonical consumer standard lives in home-network's ci-release-standard.
